content:
# James Chapter 5 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Date:** 2026-03-06
**Translations compared:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), pages 264-265

---

## Summary Statistics
- **Total disagreements logged:** 31
- **Verses with full agreement:** 2 (v.2, v.6 — near-complete)
- **Verses with significant divergence:** vv.1, 4, 5, 9, 11, 16, 17
- **Most common disagreement type:** Vocabulary/word choice (17), Phrasing/structure (11), Address form (3)

---

## Disagreements by Verse

### Verse 1
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | Vocabulary | "howling" (participle) | "howl" (imperative) | "wail" | "howl" | LOGOS best balances force and English grammar; OLOLYZONTES is a rare prophetic cry — "wail" (PNEUMA) softens it |
| 2 | Phrasing | "Come now, the rich ones" | "Come now, you rich" | "Now listen, you wealthy people" | "Come now, you rich" | AGE NUN is a standard Greek interjection; EMET/LOGOS preserve it directly; PNEUMA paraphrases |
| 3 | Vocabulary | "miseries...coming upon you" | "miseries that are coming upon you" | "miseries that are about to overtake you" | "miseries that are coming upon you" | PNEUMA's "about to overtake" adds urgency not explicit in the present participle EPERCHOMENAIS |

### Verse 3
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 4 | Vocabulary | "rusted" | "corroded" | "corroded" | "corroded" | 2-1 majority; "corroded" correctly applies to both gold and silver |
| 5 | Vocabulary | "stored up treasure" | "stored up treasure" | "hoarded wealth" | "stored up treasure" | EMET/LOGOS agree; PNEUMA's "hoarded" imports negative judgment beyond ETHESAURISATE |

### Verse 4
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 6 | Vocabulary | "workers" | "laborers" | "workers" | "laborers" | LOGOS preferred for agricultural context; ERGATON covers both senses |
| 7 | Vocabulary | "Lord of Sabaoth" | "Lord of Sabaoth" | "Lord of Hosts" | "Lord of Sabaoth" | EMET/LOGOS retain the Hebrew loanword as found in the Greek manuscript; PNEUMA translates it |
| 8 | Vocabulary | "having harvested" | "mowed" | "harvested" | "harvested" | LOGOS's "mowed" overly specific; AMHSANTAS means reaped/harvested broadly |

### Verse 5
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 9 | Vocabulary | "nourished the hearts of you" | "fattened your hearts" | "fattened yourselves" | "fattened your hearts" | ETHREPSATE TAS KARDIAS: "nourished" (EMET) too positive; PNEUMA drops "hearts" (TAS KARDIAS) |
| 10 | Phrasing | "in a day of slaughter" | "in a day of slaughter" | "for the day of slaughter" | "in a day of slaughter" | EN HMERA = "in a day of"; PNEUMA's "for" changes from temporal to purposive |

### Verse 6
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 11 | Vocabulary | "the righteous one" | "the righteous one" | "the innocent person" | "the righteous one" | TON DIKAION = "the righteous one"; PNEUMA shifts to "innocent" which is a different semantic field |

### Verse 7
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 12 | Address | "brothers" | "brothers" | "brothers and sisters" | "brothers" | ADELPHOI: EMET/LOGOS translate the masculine plural directly; PNEUMA adds inclusive gloss |
| 13 | Vocabulary | "precious fruit of the earth" | "precious fruit of the earth" | "precious harvest from the land" | "precious fruit of the earth" | KARPON = fruit; PNEUMA interprets as "harvest" and changes "earth" to "land" |

### Verse 8
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 14 | Phrasing | "establish the hearts of you" | "establish your hearts" | "Stand firm in your hearts" | "establish your hearts" | STHRIXATE = establish/strengthen; PNEUMA paraphrases as "stand firm" |

### Verse 9
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 15 | Vocabulary | "groan" | "grumble" | "grumble" | "grumble" | 2-1 majority; STENAZETE in context of mutual complaint better rendered "grumble" |
| 16 | Phrasing | "the Judge before the doors stands" | "the Judge stands before the doors" | "the Judge is standing right at the door" | "the Judge stands before the doors" | EMET preserves Greek word order; LOGOS normalizes; PNEUMA dramatizes and changes plural "doors" to singular "door" |

### Verse 10
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 17 | Word order | "an example take, brothers" | "as an example...brothers, take" | "take the prophets...as an example" | "as an example...brothers, take the prophets" | LOGOS best balances Greek structure with English readability |

### Verse 11
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 18 | Name | "Iob" | "Job" | "Job" | "Job" | 2-1 majority; standard English convention for IOB |
| 19 | Vocabulary | "full of compassion" | "very compassionate" | "deeply compassionate" | "very compassionate" | POLYSPLAGCHNOS: three distinct renderings; LOGOS balances accuracy with register |
| 20 | Vocabulary | "purpose of the Lord" | "purpose of the Lord" | "outcome the Lord brought about" | "purpose of the Lord" | TELOS: EMET/LOGOS agree on "purpose"; PNEUMA interprets as "outcome" with expansion |

### Verse 12
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 21 | Phrasing | "before all things, brothers of me" | "above all things, my brothers" | "above all, my brothers and sisters" | "above all, my brothers" | PRO PANTON: EMET hyper-literal; LOGOS/PNEUMA normalize; address per standing decision |

### Verse 13
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 22 | Phrasing | "Let him sing psalms" | "Let him sing psalms" | "They should sing songs of praise" | "Let him sing psalms" | PSALLETW = sing psalms; PNEUMA generalizes and shifts to plural pronoun |
| 23 | Pronoun | "Let him" (singular) | "Let him" (singular) | "They should" (plural) | "Let him" | Greek uses singular TIS...PROSEUECHESTHW; EMET/LOGOS preserve; PNEUMA shifts to gender-neutral plural |

### Verse 14
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 24 | Vocabulary | "assembly" | "church" | "church" | "church" | EKKLHSIAS: EMET uses base meaning "assembly"; LOGOS/PNEUMA use conventional NT rendering |

### Verse 15
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 25 | Phrasing | "the prayer of the faith" | "the prayer of faith" | "the prayer offered in faith" | "the prayer of faith" | PNEUMA expands with "offered in"; EMET retains the Greek article; LOGOS balances |
| 26 | Verb form | "will save...will raise" | "shall save...shall raise" | "will restore...will raise" | "shall save...shall raise" | LOGOS's "shall" preserves formal promissory tone; PNEUMA's "restore" goes beyond SWSEI |

### Verse 16
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 27 | Phrasing | "Much avails the prayer of a righteous one, working effectually" | "The fervent prayer of a righteous man avails much" | "The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and produces wonderful results" | "The effective prayer of a righteous person avails much" | Major three-way split on DEHSIS ENERGOUMENH DIKAIOU; consensus balances structure and clarity |

### Verse 17
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 28 | Name | "Elias" | "Elijah" | "Elijah" | "Elijah" | 2-1 majority; standard English for HLIAS |
| 29 | Phrasing | "a man of like nature to us" | "a man of like nature with us" | "a human being just like us" | "a man of like nature with us" | HOMOIOPATHHS: EMET/LOGOS preserve formal structure; PNEUMA paraphrases for accessibility |
| 30 | Phrasing | "with prayer he prayed for it not to rain" | "he prayed earnestly that it might not rain" | "he prayed earnestly that it would not rain" | "he prayed earnestly that it would not rain" | PROSEUCH PROSHUXATO: EMET mirrors Hebraic cognate accusative literally; LOGOS/PNEUMA render idiomatically |

### Verse 18
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 31 | Vocabulary | "sprouted the fruit of it" | "brought forth its fruit" | "produced its crops" | "brought forth its fruit" | EBLASTHSEN: "sprouted" (EMET) vivid but narrow; "brought forth" (LOGOS) standard; "crops" (PNEUMA) too interpretive |

### Verse 19
(Address disagreement same as v.7 — "brothers" vs. "brothers and sisters"; counted above)

### Verse 20
| # | Category | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 32 | Phrasing | "a soul from death" | "a soul from death" | "that person's soul from death" | "a soul from death" | Sinaiticus lacks AUTOU; EMET/LOGOS preserve textual ambiguity; PNEUMA specifies |
| 33 | Vocabulary | "the one having turned back" | "the one who turns" | "whoever turns" | "the one who turns" | LOGOS best renders the participle in natural English while preserving the definite article |

---

## Recurring Patterns

### 1. ADELPHOI Address (vv. 7, 9, 10, 12, 19)
PNEUMA consistently renders ADELPHOI as "brothers and sisters" while EMET and LOGOS retain "brothers." SOPHIA follows the majority and the direct Greek form in all instances.

### 2. Pronoun Style (vv. 13-15)
PNEUMA systematically uses gender-neutral plural pronouns ("they/them") where the Greek has singular masculine forms (TIS + third-person singular imperative). EMET and LOGOS preserve the singular. SOPHIA follows the Greek grammar.

### 3. Name Transliteration (vv. 11, 17)
EMET consistently transliterates Greek name forms (Iob, Elias) while LOGOS and PNEUMA use conventional English forms (Job, Elijah). SOPHIA follows the majority English convention.

### 4. "Lord of Sabaoth" vs. "Lord of Hosts" (v. 4)
A theological translation decision: EMET and LOGOS retain the untranslated Hebrew loanword as it appears in the Greek text; PNEUMA translates the meaning. SOPHIA preserves the loanword following the manuscript.
